title: juniperus deppeana subsp. sperryi
intro: Juniperus deppeana subsp. sperryi, also known as Sperry's juniper, is a subspecies of coniferous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory issues. The essential oils of Sperry's juniper have been shown to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and well-being. Sperry's juniper can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
